summary.headline: June closed $970K on the spring buying peak; HIH softened seasonally to 76; MQL/SQL inflated by an offline lifecycle import.
summary.body: June's story is revenue: $970,129 closed won across 34 deals (63% win rate by count, 83% by dollar) — the strongest month this fiscal year and on-cycle for the spring-summer district buying peak, though 82% of it came from two deals (Idea Public Schools $641K, Yonkers $160K). Genuine demand softened as expected heading into summer: HIH came in at 76, down 22% from May's 101, which is seasonal, not a signal problem. MQL and SQL totals are inflated by a large offline lifecycle import (95% OFFLINE-sourced, no UTM); the product-tagged funnel is roughly flat versus May (IJ 54 HIH, 30 SQL). Two watch items: UTM completeness collapsed to 2.1%, and Medium-segment deals lost the month (3 won vs 5 lost, $114K in losses).

signals.label: MQL/SQL inflated by offline lifecycle import
Medium segment: 3 won vs 5 lost, -$105K net

kpis.label: MQL → SQL Conv.
kpis.sub: Inflated by offline lifecycle import — not a genuine quality signal

current.narrative: Week of Jul 13: 27 new MQLs, 23 SQLs, 23 Opps — raw velocity up WoW but MQL→SQL at 85% (23/27) is inflated by ongoing offline lifecycle migration, consistent with prior weeks. HIH at 5 new entries — deepest summer low of the season, expected. Closed-won July MTD: $704K across 44 deals, pacing well for a summer month. Open pipeline: 151 deals (110 district, 41 school). No competitor site signals this run — sites returned 403.
